strings: WORKERS COMPENSATION AND EMPLOYERS LIABILITY INSURANCE POLICY
Item 1. Named Insured and Mailing Address
From 08/01/2025 to 08/01/2026, 12:01 A.M. Standard Time at the mailing address shown above.
Description: General contracting for commercial and institutional construction — healthcare facilities, educational
buildings, and corporate offices throughout Western Pennsylvania
Forms and endorsements applying to this policy at the time of issue are listed in the Endorsement Schedule.
C. Examination Of Your Books And Records
F. Transfer Of Your Rights And Duties Under This Policy
Part One. PART ONE — WORKERS COMPENSATION INSURANCE
Part Two. PART TWO — EMPLOYERS LIABILITY INSURANCE
Part Three. PART THREE — OTHER STATES INSURANCE
Part Four. PART FOUR — YOUR DUTIES IF INJURY OCCURS

The first Named Insured shown in the Declarations may cancel this policy by mailing or delivering to the Company advance written notice of cancellation. The Company may cancel this policy by mailing or delivering to the first Named Insured written notice of cancellation at least 10 days before the effective date of cancellation if cancellation is for nonpayment of premium, or 30 days before the effective date of cancellation if cancellation is for any other reason.

This policy contains all the agreements between you and us concerning the insurance afforded. The first Named Insured shown in the Declarations is authorized to make changes in the terms of this policy with our consent. This policy's terms can be amended or waived only by endorsement issued by the Company and made a part of this policy.
The Company may examine and audit your books and records as they relate to this policy at any time during the policy period and up to three years afterward.

The Company has the right but is not obligated to make inspections and surveys at any time. Neither the right to make inspections and surveys nor the making thereof nor any report thereon shall constitute an undertaking, on behalf of or for the benefit of the insured or others, to determine or warrant that such property or operations are safe or healthful, or are in compliance with any law, rule or regulation.

The first Named Insured shown in the Declarations is responsible for the payment of all premiums. The Company will compute all premiums for this policy based on our rates, rules, and forms in effect at the inception of this policy. Premium shown is an estimate. The final premium will be determined after this policy expires by using the actual, not the estimated, premium basis and the proper classifications and rates that lawfully apply to the business and activities conducted during the policy period.

Your rights and duties under this policy may not be transferred without our written consent except in the case of death of an individual named insured. If you die, your rights and duties will be transferred to your legal representative but only while acting within the scope of duties as your legal representative.

The Company will pay promptly when due the benefits required of you by the workers compensation law. Part One applies to bodily injury by accident or bodily injury by disease. Bodily injury includes resulting death. Bodily injury by accident must occur during the policy period. Bodily injury by disease must be caused or aggravated by the conditions of your employment. The employee's last day of last exposure to the conditions causing or aggravating such bodily injury by disease must occur during the policy period.

The Company will pay all sums you legally must pay as damages because of bodily injury to your employees, provided the bodily injury is covered by this Employers Liability Insurance. The damages the Company will pay, where recovery is permitted by law, include damages for which you are liable to a third party by reason of a claim or suit against you by that third party to recover the damages claimed against such third party as a result of injury to your employee; damages for care and loss of services; and damages for consequential bodily injury to a spouse, child, parent, brother or sister of the injured employee.

If you begin work in any one of the states listed in Item 3.C. of the Information Page after the effective date of this policy, Part Three applies unless you already have insurance or are self-insured for such state.
PART FOUR — YOUR DUTIES IF INJURY OCCURS
1. Provide for immediate medical and other services required by the workers compensation law.
2. Give us or our agent the names and addresses of the injured persons and of witnesses, and other information we may need.
3. Promptly give us all notices, demands and legal papers related to the injury, claim, proceeding or suit.
4. Cooperate with us and assist us, as we may request, in the investigation, settlement or defense of any claim, proceeding or suit.
5. Do nothing after an injury occurs that would interfere with our right to recover from others.
6. Do not voluntarily make payments, assume obligations or incur expenses, other than at your own cost.

All premium for this policy will be determined by our manuals of rules, rates, rating plans and classifications. All information required for premium computation will be obtained in accordance with our rules.

Item 4 of the Information Page shows the rate and premium basis for certain business or work classifications applicable to this policy. These classifications were assigned based on an estimate of the exposures you would have during the policy period.

Premium for each work classification is determined by multiplying a rate times a premium basis. Remuneration means money or substitutes for money. Premium bases include payroll and all other remuneration paid or payable during the policy period for the services of employees engaged in work covered by this policy.
You will pay all premium when due. You will also pay us premium as exchange for the insurance afforded during the policy period.

The premium shown on the Information Page, schedules, and endorsements is an estimate. The final premium will be determined after this policy ends by using the actual, not the estimated, premium basis and the proper classifications and rates that lawfully apply to the business and work covered by this policy. If the final premium is more than the estimated premium, you must pay us the balance. If it is less, we will return the excess to you.
You will keep records of information needed to compute premium. You will provide us with copies of those records when we ask for them.

You will let us examine and audit all your records that relate to this policy. These examinations and audits may be made during the policy period and up to three years after the policy period ends. Information developed by audit will be used to determine final premium.
